results.description: TRUST FOR PUBLIC LAND (TPL) WILL LEAD THE DEVELOPMENT OF A DATA VISUALIZATION HUB AS WELL AS LEVERAGING ITS NETWORK, MARKETING TEAM, AND COMMUNICATIONS EXPERTISE TO SUPPORT OUTREACH PLANS AND STRATEGIES TO HELP REACH RURAL AND REMOTE AREAS. TRUST FOR PUBLIC LAND WILL ALSO SUPPORT MEASUREMENT AND EVALUATION EFFORTS. TPL WILL SUPPORT INSTITUTE FOR SUSTAINABLE COMMUNITIES IN COLLECTING AND ORGANIZING DATA FROM SUBGRANTEES AND REGIONAL GRANTMAKERS FOR USE IN THE DATA VISUALIZATION HUB. TPL WILL DELIVER GRANTEE SUCCESS SUPPORT FOR OUR PARTNERSHIP’S SUBGRANTEES THROUGH A TEAM OF SPECIALIZED EXPERTS IN WEATHER IMPACTS, HEALTH, COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, CONSERVATION FINANCE, PARKS AND CONSERVATION, COMMUNITY SCHOOLYARDS, HISTORY AND CULTURE, LEARNING AND EVALUATION, FEDERAL LAND MANAGEMENT, COMMUNITY STRATEGIES, AND FEDERAL GRANT COMPLIANCE.

EMERALD CITIES COLLECTIVE (ECC) WILL PROVIDE EXPERTISE FOR APPLICATION INTAKE AND EVALUATION PROCESSES. THEY WILL ALSO LEAD THE PARTICIPATORY GOVERNANCE PROCESS. ECC WILL REVIEW APPLICATIONS WITH A MERIT-BASED FRAMEWORK ON A ROLLING BASIS, PROVIDE ONGOING MANAGEMENT OF THE ADVISORY COUNCIL, PROVIDE STRATEGIC COMMUNICATION SUPPORT FOR TARGET AUDIENCES, AND PARTICIPATE IN APPLICABLE PROJECT MEETINGS.

RIVER NETWORK WILL PROVIDE OVERALL LEADERSHIP FOR THE SOLUTIONS FOR COMMUNITY RESILIENCE (SCR) THROUGH THE PROJECT LEADERSHIP COMMITTEE AND SUPPORTING STORYTELLING WORKGROUP’S ACTIVITIES AND PROJECTS. RIVER NETWORK STAFF WILL ALSO DELIVER SERVICES IN SUPPORT OF SCR GRANTMAKING; PROVIDE CAPACITY-BUILDING SUPPORT FOR SCR GRANTEES AND REGIONAL GRANTMAKERS; AND SUPPORT A VARIETY OF PROJECT FUNCTIONS BY ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING IN VARIOUS WORKING GROUPS.

USDN WILL BUILD LOCAL GOVERNMENT UNDERSTANDING AND CAPACITY TO ALIGN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT AND RESILIENCE PRIORITIES; FACILITATE RELATIONSHIPS BETWEEN CBOS AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS; ELEVATE STORIES AND LESSONS OF SUCCESSFUL GOVERNMENT-COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIP THROUGH PEER LEARNING AND COMMUNICATION CHANNELS. USDN WILL ALSO PROVIDE RESOURCES ON APPLYING FOR AND RECEIVING FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE FOR LOCAL GOVERNMENT SUSTAINABILITY OFFICES TO BE A HOST OFFICE IN THE USDN FELLOWS PROGRAM. USDN WILL WORK WITH ISC AND OTHER PARTNERS TO FACILITATE TRUST BUILDING AND CO-CREATION BETWEEN LOCAL GOVERNMENTS AND CBOS.

GWUSA WILL LEAD THE DEVELOPMENT OF CAPACITY BUILDING AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE FOR GRANTEES. GWUSA WILL ALSO PROVIDE EXPERTISE TO SUPPORT THE APPLICATION INTAKE AND EVALUATION PROCESS AS WELL AS SUPPORTING THE DEVELOPMENT OF OUTREACH AND EVALUATION MATERIALS. IN ADDITION, GWUSA WILL SUPPORT DEVELOPMENT OF THE DATA VISUALIZATION HUB. GROUNDWORK USA WILL LEAD THE CO-DEVELOPMENT OF, DELIVER, AND REPORT ON A CAPACITY BUILDING (CB) PROGRAM FOR SCR GRANTEES IN COLLABORATION WITH NATIONAL PARTNERS THROUGH A COMBINATION OF AN ONBOARDING PROCESS, 1:1 GRANTEE SUPPORT, SKILL-BUILDING AND EDUCATIONAL WORKSHOPS, AND OTHER PROGRAMMING OPPORTUNITIES FOR PEER-LEARNING. SERVICES PROVIDED WILL BE CENTERED ON THE INTEGRATION OF COMMUNITY-CENTERED, COMMUNITY-DRIVEN STRATEGIES, PRINCIPLES, AND BEST PRACTICES TO SUPPORT SCR GRANTEE PROJECT SUCCESS.
